things.name: The Falkirk Wheel (Rotating Boat Lift)
things.description: The world's only rotating boat lift — a stunning feat of engineering connecting the Forth & Clyde and Union Canals with a 35-metre rotation

things.name: The Kelpies (Horse Head Sculptures)
things.description: Andy Scott's 30-metre horse head sculptures — the world's largest equine statues, celebrating the horse's role in Scottish industry

things.name: Callendar House & Park
things.description: Grand French château-style mansion in 170-acre parkland with a Georgian kitchen museum and working bakehouse

things.name: Rough Castle (Antonine Wall, UNESCO)
things.description: Best-preserved fort along the UNESCO-listed Antonine Wall — the northernmost frontier of the Roman Empire built in AD 142

things.name: Bo'ness & Kinneil Steam Railway
things.description: Scotland's largest collection of railway heritage with a restored steam railway, vintage rolling stock and Scotland's Railway Exhibition

things.name: Falkirk Steeple & Town Centre
things.description: The iconic 1814 steeple at the heart of Falkirk's historic high street, surrounded by independent shops and cafes

things.name: Helix Park (Kelpies Park)
things.description: Expansive park surrounding the Kelpies with a lagoon, adventure play areas, cycling routes and seasonal events

things.name: Stirling Castle (Day Trip)
things.description: One of Scotland's greatest castles — the former seat of the Stuart monarchs with a spectacular Renaissance Royal Palace

things.name: Kinneil Estate & Museum
things.description: Historic estate where James Watt developed his steam engine — a ruined palace, Roman fortlet and local museum

things.name: Grangemouth Chemical & History Museum
things.description: The story of Scotland's petrochemical industry and the historic Carron Ironworks where Napoleon's carronades were cast
